selected_important_turn.text: okay I will give you 3 firewood 1 food and 1 water because I too have family,there two old people and 1 child.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is a major concession: you give away your top-priority item (firewood) fully. It materially shifts the division of scarce value and signals a willingness to accommodate the opponent unconditionally. That concession both determines the resource split and makes later bargaining about food/water more one-sided.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although cooperative, it's suboptimal because you surrender your highest-valued resource without securing reciprocal value or tradeoffs. The explanation about family increases sympathy but doesn't extract any reciprocal commitment. This made the deal less fair for you and left room for the opponent to keep pressing for more.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Rather than conceding all three firewood immediately, make a conditional or package offer that protects some of your top value or asks for reciprocity (e.g., trade firewood for additional water/food, or offer 2 firewood + extra food/water). Probe which item is truly critical and propose explicit give-get tradeoffs so concessions are balanced.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase — by packaging tradeoffs you can create value and avoid wasteful unilateral concessions
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — asking for reciprocity prevents one-sided outcomes and yields a more balanced split
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: short-term may decrease (you'll push harder), but medium-term ease increases because agreements will be more stable and acceptable
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — being willing to concede is positive, but doing so conditionally preserves dignity and trust (reduces resentment)

dialogue.text: hai friend, me and my team going to trip on some snow fall place i want some help from you
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yeah sure. I do my level best .
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: okay friend. In my team old age sicked peoples and kids are there so i need some extra package.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay friend. Which item do you need the most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 3 pack of fire wood and 2 pack of food and 2 pack of water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: But If I will give you these items I don't get enough package friend.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: i understand friend, we going to a snow fall place so we need fire wood and sicked and kids are in my team so i need food and water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay I will give you 2 firewood 2 food and 1 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: food and water is ok, but i must need 3 pack of fire wood friend
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: okay I will give you 3 firewood 1 food and 1 water because I too have family,there two old people and 1 child.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade 2 firewood for 1 extra food package. This way, I still secure some firewood while giving you more food, which aligns with our needs. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that trade, but I'd prefer to keep 2 firewood for myself. Let's split the remaining 1 firewood and 2 food packages evenly. That way, I secure my priority needs while keeping things fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your preference for firewood, but I need to ensure I get enough for cooking and boiling water. Let’s try 1 firewood for 1 food package. That way, I still get some firewood, and you get extra food. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with trading 1 firewood for 1 food package, but I need to keep at least 2 firewood for myself. Let's split the remaining 2 firewood and 2 food packages evenly. That way, I secure my priority needs while keeping things fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need to prioritize firewood for cooking and boiling water. Let’s agree on 2 firewood for me, 1 firewood for you, and split the remaining 2 food packages evenly. This way, I secure my high-priority needs while keeping things fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood for my priority needs. Let’s finalize: 2 firewood for me, 1 firewood for you, and split the 2 food packages evenly. That way, I secure my high-priority needs while keeping things fair.
